ddls:
CREATE TABLE "adults" (
  "id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each adult */,
  "age" INTEGER /* Age of the adult */,
  "workclass_id" INTEGER /* Work class of the adult */,
  "fnlwgt" INTEGER /* Final weight (number of people the entry represents) */,
  "education_id" INTEGER /* Education level of the adult */,
  "education_num" INTEGER /* Numerical code for education level */,
  "marital_status_id" INTEGER /* Marital status of the adult */,
  "occupation_id" INTEGER /* Occupation of the adult */,
  "relationship" TEXT /* Relationship status of the adult */,
  "race_id" INTEGER /* Race of the adult */,
  "sex" TEXT /* Sex of the adult */,
  "capital_gain" INTEGER /* Capital gain for the adult */,
  "capital_loss" INTEGER /* Capital loss for the adult */,
  "hours_per_week" INTEGER /* Hours worked per week by the adult */,
  "country_id" INTEGER /* Country of origin for the adult */,
  "salary_id" INTEGER /* Salary range of the adult */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address for contact purposes */,
  "phone" TEXT /* Phone number for contact purposes */,
  "address" TEXT /* Physical address for location-based analysis */,
  "date_of_birth" TEXT /* Date of birth for age verification */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the record was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the record was last updated */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Flag to indicate if the record is active */,
  "source_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key to the data_sources table to track the origin of the data */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_adults_race_id FOREIGN KEY ("race_id") REFERENCES races ("race_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_adults_occupation_id FOREIGN KEY ("occupation_id") REFERENCES occupations ("occupation_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_adults_country_id FOREIGN KEY ("country_id") REFERENCES countries ("country_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_adults_workclass_id FOREIGN KEY ("workclass_id") REFERENCES workclasses ("workclass_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_adults_education_id FOREIGN KEY ("education_id") REFERENCES education_levels ("education_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_adults_marital_status_id FOREIGN KEY ("marital_status_id") REFERENCES marital_statuses ("marital_status_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_adults_salary_id FOREIGN KEY ("salary_id") REFERENCES salaries ("salary_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_adults_source_id FOREIGN KEY ("source_id") REFERENCES data_sources ("source_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "workclasses" (
  "workclass_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each work class */,
  "workclass_name" TEXT /* Name of the work class */,
  "description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the work class */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Flag to indicate if the work class is active */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("workclass_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "education_levels" (
  "education_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each education level */,
  "education_level" TEXT /* Name of the education level */,
  "education_code" INTEGER /* Numerical code for the education level */,
  "description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the education level */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Flag to indicate if the education level is active */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("education_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "marital_statuses" (
  "marital_status_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each marital status */,
  "marital_status_name" TEXT /* Name of the marital status */,
  "description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the marital status */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Flag to indicate if the marital status is active */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("marital_status_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "occupations" (
  "occupation_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each occupation */,
  "occupation_name" TEXT /* Name of the occupation */,
  "description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the occupation */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Flag to indicate if the occupation is active */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("occupation_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "races" (
  "race_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each race */,
  "race_name" TEXT /* Name of the race */,
  "description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the race */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Flag to indicate if the race is active */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("race_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "countries" (
  "country_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each country */,
  "country_name" TEXT /* Name of the country */,
  "description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the country */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Flag to indicate if the country is active */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("country_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "salaries" (
  "salary_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each salary range */,
  "salary_range" TEXT /* Range of the salary */,
  "description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the salary range */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Flag to indicate if the salary range is active */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("salary_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_sources" (
  "source_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data source */,
  "source_name" TEXT /* Name of the data source */,
  "source_description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the data source */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Flag to indicate if the data source is active */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("source_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role */,
  "role_description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the role */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Flag to indicate if the role is active */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username for the user */,
  "password" TEXT /* Password for the user (hashed) */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address for the user */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Role of the user */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Flag to indicate if the user is active */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_users_role_id FOREIGN KEY ("role_id") REFERENCES user_roles ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_access" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access record */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* User ID */,
  "table_name" TEXT /* Name of the table the user has access to */,
  "read_permission" INTEGER /* Flag to indicate read permission */,
  "write_permission" INTEGER /* Flag to indicate write permission */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Flag to indicate if the access record is active */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id")
)
